title: Detachable panels are evil!
author: Apollon-Theos
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->

Manual sort panels or tabs, with shift at 1 pixel up or down - panel detached from main window. Attach back not fast and not from first attempt. Time from work lost, to attach panel or tab back. Badly.

## Expected Behavior
Sorting without detach panel or tab.

## Current Behavior
<!--- If describing a bug, tell us what happens instead of the expected behavior -->
<!--- If suggesting a change/improvement, explain the d...

iterations.description: The issue highlights a critical usability problem ('evil' behavior) with detachable panels, which likely stems from the implementation of the WeifenLuo.WinFormsUI.Docking library.

comments.author: Apollon-Theos
comments.snippet:
Now, work with v 1.76.20, by reorder panels, detach one panel.
Try to attach it again, attach as NEW panel-bar, divide screen by half vertically. All opened connection change screen resolution and auto resize all opened windows inside connection to fit to new "screen resolution". 
Lost many time to restore work environment.
If I need separated windows with connection, I can use MSTSC not mRemoteNG. mRemoteNG aggregate many RDP-connections inside one window, this is main feature on mRemoteNG....

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The issue highlights a critical usability problem ('evil' behavior) with detachable panels, which likely stems from the implementation of the WeifenLuo.WinFormsUI.Docking library.
Approach: Investigate the usage and configuration of WeifenLuo.WinFormsUI.Docking, focusing on panel lifecycle, serialization/deserialization, and user interaction patterns that lead to reported 'evil' behavior. Identify specific scenarios where panels misbehave and implement fixes or refactor the docking logic to improve stability and user experience.
